LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EOK MARKETING, LLC DBA MARINE ESSENTIALS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 26, 2016 AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, PER THE AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EOK MARKETING, LLC DBA MARINE ESSENTIALS' AMENDED MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN ITS ENTITLEMENT TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT UNDER BOTH THE MANDATORY AND DISCRETIONARY PROVISIONS OF CCP 437(B). MOREOVER, CALIFORNIA'S STRONG PUBLIC POLICY FAVORING RESOLUTION OF DISPUTES ON THEIR MERITS SUPPORTS THE GRANTING OF THIS MOTION. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED ON THE GROUNDS OF INSUFFICIENT EVIDENTIARY SUPPORT FOR THE INCURRENCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Amended Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Mar-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. THOMAS A. BURG, ESQ. of Bialson, Bergen & Schwab, Tel No. 650-857-9500 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. NORA E. WETZEL, ESQ. of Sedgwick LLP, Tel No. 415-781-7900 appearing for Defendant EOK Marketing, LLC dba Marine Essentials.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court modifies its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: DEFENDANT EOK MARKETING, LLC DBA MARINE ESSENTIALS' Amended Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default is granted. Defendant has shown its entitlement to set aside the default under both the mandatory and discretionary provisions of CCP 437(b). Moreover, California's strong public policy favoring resolution of disputes on their merits supports the granting of this motion. Plaintiff's request for fees and costs is denied on the grounds of insufficient evidentiary support for the incurrence. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
